text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: The river swells from the heavy rainfall.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
As Air Shakur observes, she discusses the finitude
of stats.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Every river has a capacity—an upper limit for how
much water it can hold before the excess
overflows. There's no way to change the limit
without changing the river itself, potentially
destroying it as it was.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The same restrictions apply to racers' growth, and
this year, many will have to wrestle their
limitations.
